skill.name: qwen-image
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Qwen-Image-2.0 (fast), Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro (professional text rendering). Capabilities: text-to-image, multi-image editing, complex text rendering. Triggers: qwen image, qwen-image, alibaba image, dashscope image, qwen image 2, qwen image pro
skill.summary: Generate images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models via inference.sh CLI with text-to-image, multi-image editing, and professional text rendering support.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation wrapper for Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 image generation models via the inference.sh CLI. All static findings are false positives: (1) external_commands detected markdown code blocks with bash examples, not actual shell execution; (2) network findings are legitimate documentation URLs; (3) cryptographic and system reconnaissance flags are scanner misinterpretations. No actual security risks identified.

high_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands in Documentation
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns at 39 locations. These are bash command examples in markdown code blocks (e.g., 'infsh login', 'infsh app run...'), not actual code execution. The skill uses the infsh CLI tool which is installed separately.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code fences containing bash command examples are not executable code. This is documentation for the inference.sh CLI tool.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Hardcoded URL' patterns at 8 locations. These are legitimate documentation URLs (inference.sh docs, example placeholder URLs) used for user guidance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to inference.sh documentation or example placeholder domains (example.com). No external data transmission occurs from this skill.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines. This is a scanner misinterpretation - no cryptographic algorithms are present in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains only markdown documentation. No cryptographic code or algorithms exist in this file.

high_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'System reconnaissance' at line 118. This is a false positive - the line contains a negative_prompt parameter for image generation ('low resolution, blurry, deformed, oversaturated'), not system scanning.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 'negative_prompt' is an image generation parameter specifying what to avoid in generated images, completely unrelated to system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Create stunning images from text prompts using Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models with support for complex text rendering, multi-image editing, and customizable resolutions.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Qwen-Image-2.0 or Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro models
Create multiple images in a single request (1-6 images)
Edit images using multi-reference prompts with up to 3 reference images
Render complex text in images using the Pro model with precise control
Customize output with specific resolutions (512x512 to 2048x2048), seeds, and negative prompts
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed separately
Requires an inference.sh account with API credentials
Total pixels must be between 512x512 and 2048x2048

use_cases.title: Quick Image Generation
use_cases.description: Generate single or multiple images from text descriptions for creative projects, social media, or prototyping.

use_cases.title: Text-Heavy Designs
use_cases.description: Create professional posters, banners, and marketing materials with accurate text rendering using the Pro model.

use_cases.title: Image Editing and Compositing
use_cases.description: Edit existing images or create composites using multiple reference images and natural language prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image with this prompt: [YOUR PROMPT DESCRIPTION]
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple text-to-image generation for any subject

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 variations of: [YOUR PROMPT]. Use different compositions and styles.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Size and Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [WIDTH]x[HEIGHT] image with: [YOUR PROMPT]. Style: [photorealistic/illustration/anime/etc]
prompt_templates.scenario: Specific dimensions and artistic style requirements

prompt_templates.title: Text Rendering Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster using Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro with the title "[YOUR TITLE]" in [COLOR] [FONT STYLE] at the top. Include subtitle "[SUBTITLE]" below. Background: [DESCRIPTION]. Use prompt_extend: false for precise control.

output_examples.input: Generate a serene mountain landscape at sunset
output_examples.output: Returns an array of generated PNG images with metadata including dimensions and count

output_examples.input: Create a minimalist logo for a coffee shop with 4 variations
output_examples.output: Returns 4 different logo designs as PNG images

output_examples.input: Generate a poster with title 'Summer Sale!' using the Pro model with 1024x1536 resolution
output_examples.output: Returns a professionally rendered poster with accurate text placement

best_practices: Use the Pro model (qwen-image-2-pro) when text accuracy is important in your images
Set prompt_extend: false when you need precise control over the generated output
Specify width, height, and negative_prompt parameters to refine your results
anti_patterns: Do not exceed 800 characters in your prompt or 500 characters in negative_prompt
Do not request resolutions outside the 512x512 to 2048x2048 pixel range
Do not use more than 3 reference images for multi-image editing

faq.question: What is the difference between Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro?
faq.answer: The Pro model offers professional-grade text rendering, finer realism, and enhanced semantic adherence. Use the standard model for fast general use and Pro for text-heavy designs.

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run: npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools. Then authenticate with: infsh login

faq.question: Can I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, use the reference_images parameter with up to 3 input images and describe the edits you want in the prompt.

faq.question: How do I get reproducible results?
faq.answer: Set the seed parameter to a specific integer value (0-2147483647) to generate the same image from the same prompt.

faq.question: What resolution should I use?
faq.answer: Use 1024x1024 for social media, 1920x1080 for banners, or 1024x1536 for posters. Total pixels must stay within 512x512 to 2048x2048.

faq.question: How do I control what NOT to include in the image?
faq.answer: Use the negative_prompt parameter to specify elements you want to avoid, such as 'low resolution, blurry, deformed'.
